content.text: Readiness probe fails with "Authentication failed". Let me examine the health-check script and the override configmap.

input.command: echo "===health-check-script==="; kubectl get cm health-check-script -n mongodb -o jsonpath='{.data.health-check\.sh}' 2>&1; echo; echo "===health-overrides==="; kubectl get cm health-overrides -n mongodb -o yaml 2>&1 | sed -n '1,40p'; echo "===secret==="; kubectl get secret health-user-password -n mongodb -o yaml 2>&1 | sed -n '1,20p'
input.description: Inspect health check script and config

content.content:
===health-check-script===
#!/bin/sh
set -e
PW_FILE="/etc/health/health-password"
OVERRIDE="/etc/health/overrides/${POD_NAME}"
if [ -f "$OVERRIDE" ]; then
  HEALTH_PW="$(cat "$OVERRIDE")"
else
  HEALTH_PW="$(cat "$PW_FILE")"
fi
mongosh --quiet "mongodb://health-user:${HEALTH_PW}@localhost:27017/admin" --eval 'db.hello().ok' >/dev/null

===health-overrides===
apiVersion: v1
data:
  mongodb-replica-1: legacyHealthPass123
kind: ConfigMap
metadata:
  annotations:
    kubectl.kubernetes.io/last-applied-configuration: |
      {"apiVersion":"v1","data":{"mongodb-replica-1":"legacyHealthPass123"},"kind":"ConfigMap","metadata":{"annotations":{},"name":"health-overrides","namespace":"mongodb"}}
  creationTimestamp: "2026-06-18T14:42:30Z"
  name: health-overrides
  namespace: mongodb
  resourceVersion: "335204"
  uid: 913fc9c3-592b-4a48-b9b6-f7ffed2dd6b9
===secret===

  password: aGVhbHRocGFzczEyMw==
kind: Secret
metadata:
  annotations:
    kubectl.kubernetes.io/last-applied-configuration: |
      {"apiVersion":"v1","kind":"Secret","metadata":{"annotations":{},"name":"health-user-password","namespace":"mongodb"},"stringData":{"password":"healthpass123"},"type":"Opaque"}
  creationTimestamp: "2026-06-18T14:42:29Z"
  name: health-user-password
  namespace: mongodb
  resourceVersion: "335199"
  uid: 49e417ea-bdf4-4a59-9174-00f4406378be
type: Opaque

content.text: The override configmap gives `mongodb-replica-1` a stale password `legacyHealthPass123`, while the real health-user password is `healthpass123` (from the secret). Pods 0 and 2 have no override so they authenticate fine. Let me verify the actual working password before fixing.

input.command: echo "=== test real secret pw on health-user via pod-0 ==="; kubectl exec -n mongodb mongodb-replica-0 -c mongod -- mongosh --quiet "mongodb://health-user:healthpass123@localhost:27017/admin" --eval 'db.hello().ok' 2>&1; echo "=== test legacy pw ==="; kubectl exec -n mongodb mongodb-replica-0 -c mongod -- mongosh --quiet "mongodb://health-user:legacyHealthPass123@localhost:27017/admin" --eval 'db.hello().ok' 2>&1

content.content:
Exit code 1
=== test real secret pw on health-user via pod-0 ===
1
=== test legacy pw ===
MongoServerError: Authentication failed.
command terminated with exit code 1

tool_use_result:
Error: Exit code 1
=== test real secret pw on health-user via pod-0 ===
1
=== test legacy pw ===
MongoServerError: Authentication failed.
command terminated with exit code 1

content.text: Confirmed: the override password is wrong. The fix is to remove the stale override for `mongodb-replica-1` so it falls back to the correct secret password. Let me patch the configmap to drop that key.

input.command: kubectl patch cm health-overrides -n mongodb --type=json -p='[{"op":"remove","path":"/data/mongodb-replica-1"}]' 2>&1; echo "---after---"; kubectl get cm health-overrides -n mongodb -o jsonpath='{.data}' 2>&1

content.text: Override removed. Now the mounted file `/etc/health/overrides/mongodb-replica-1` will disappear after kubelet syncs the volume (up to ~60s), and the probe will fall back to the correct password. Let me poll until the pod becomes Ready.

input.command:
for i in $(seq 1 24); do
  st=$(kubectl get pod mongodb-replica-1 -n mongodb -o jsonpath='{.status.containerStatuses[0].ready}' 2>&1)
  echo "t=$((i*10))s ready=$st"
  [ "$st" = "true" ] && break
  sleep 10
done
echo "---"; kubectl get pods -n mongodb

content.text: All three pods are Ready. Now let me verify the replica set has 1 PRIMARY and 2 SECONDARY members.

input.command: kubectl exec -n mongodb mongodb-replica-0 -c mongod -- mongosh --quiet "mongodb://health-user:healthpass123@localhost:27017/admin" --eval 'rs.status().members.map(m => m.name + " : " + m.stateStr).join("\n")' 2>&1
